urdu_hindi: دل کا بڑا ہونا / दिल का बड़ा होना
patient_explanation: The heart appears larger than normal on the scan.
clinical_context: This can be seen on chest X-rays and may be related to heart strain or heart disease.
action_note: Discuss this finding with a healthcare professional.
ollama_prompt_hint: Explain cardiomegaly to a patient in simple terms. Mention that it means an enlarged heart visible on imaging, possible causes like hypertension or cardiomyopathy, and that they should consult a cardiologist.
follow_up: Echocardiogram often recommended

urdu_hindi: پھیپھڑوں کے گرد پانی / फेफड़ों के आसपास पानी
patient_explanation: There is extra fluid in the space around the lungs.
clinical_context: This can happen with infection, heart problems, or inflammation.
action_note: A doctor can explain the cause and next steps.
ollama_prompt_hint: Explain pleural effusion in simple language. Describe it as fluid buildup around the lungs, mention it can cause breathlessness, and that causes include infections, heart failure, or cancer.
follow_up: Thoracentesis may be needed for diagnosis or treatment

simple_meaning: Part of the lung is not fully open
urdu_hindi: پھیپھڑے کا حصہ بند ہونا / फेफड़े का हिस्सा बंद होना
patient_explanation: A small part of the lung is partly collapsed or not fully expanded.
clinical_context: This is commonly seen on chest imaging and may be mild or temporary.
action_note: This should be interpreted with symptoms and doctor advice.
ollama_prompt_hint: Explain atelectasis simply. Tell the patient a small portion of lung is not fully inflated, often after surgery or due to a blockage, and that breathing exercises often help.
follow_up: Breathing exercises or physiotherapy often recommended

urdu_hindi: ہڈی کا ٹوٹنا / हड्डी का टूटना
patient_explanation: A bone is cracked or broken.
clinical_context: This is commonly seen on X-rays after injury or trauma.
action_note: Medical review is needed to decide treatment.
ollama_prompt_hint: Explain a bone fracture to a patient. Describe it as a break or crack in the bone, mention types (hairline, displaced), and that treatment depends on severity ranging from casting to surgery.
follow_up: Orthopaedic consultation and immobilization

simple_meaning: Area of lung filled with infection or fluid
urdu_hindi: پھیپھڑے میں جمع ہونا / फेफड़े में भराव
patient_explanation: Part of the lung appears denser than normal, often due to infection or inflammation.
clinical_context: Commonly seen in pneumonia or lung infections.
action_note: A healthcare professional can explain the cause and treatment if needed.
ollama_prompt_hint: Explain lung consolidation in simple terms. Say that part of the lung is filled with infection, fluid, or cells instead of air, and is most commonly due to pneumonia requiring antibiotics.
follow_up: Antibiotics if infective; repeat imaging to confirm resolution

urdu_hindi: پھیپھڑے کا گرنا / फेफड़े का गिरना
patient_explanation: Air is trapped around the lung, which can cause part of the lung to collapse.
clinical_context: This may require urgent medical attention depending on the size and symptoms.
action_note: This should be reviewed urgently by a healthcare professional.
ollama_prompt_hint: Explain pneumothorax urgently but calmly. Say air has entered the space around the lung causing it to partially or fully collapse, and that it may need a needle or tube to fix.
follow_up: Urgent chest drain if large; observation if small

urdu_hindi: بافتوں میں داغ / ऊतकों में निशान
patient_explanation: There is scarring in part of the tissue seen on the scan.
clinical_context: Fibrosis can occur after inflammation or long-term disease.
action_note: A doctor can explain whether follow-up is needed.
ollama_prompt_hint: Explain lung fibrosis simply. Say that over time, inflammation or disease caused scarring in the lung tissue, which can reduce lung capacity, and that management focuses on slowing progression.
follow_up: Pulmonologist review; PFTs and HRCT

urdu_hindi: خون بہنا / रक्तस्राव
patient_explanation: Bleeding has been detected in the scanned area.
clinical_context: This may occur due to injury or medical conditions.
action_note: Urgent medical review may be needed.
ollama_prompt_hint: Explain brain hemorrhage urgently but clearly. Say there is active bleeding inside or around the brain, and depending on the size and location it may need urgent treatment or close monitoring.
follow_up: Neurosurgery review; blood pressure control

urdu_hindi: سوجن / सूजन
patient_explanation: Extra fluid is causing swelling in the tissue.
clinical_context: Edema can occur in many body areas due to inflammation or illness.
action_note: The cause should be discussed with a healthcare professional.
ollama_prompt_hint: Explain edema simply. Say excess fluid has built up in the tissue causing swelling. In the brain it can increase pressure; in limbs it may be from heart or kidney issues.
follow_up: Depends on location and severity

urdu_hindi: غیر معمولی جگہ / असामान्य क्षेत्र
patient_explanation: An area looks different from normal tissue. It does not always mean cancer.
clinical_context: This term is used when imaging shows an area needing description or follow-up.
action_note: Further evaluation may be recommended.
ollama_prompt_hint: Explain lesion gently. Tell the patient it simply means an area that looks different, does not automatically mean cancer, and further tests will clarify its nature.
follow_up: MRI with contrast or biopsy may be needed

urdu_hindi: چھوٹی گانٹھ / छोटी गांठ
patient_explanation: A small spot was seen on the scan.
clinical_context: Nodules are common and may need follow-up depending on size and appearance.
action_note: A doctor may recommend monitoring or additional imaging.
ollama_prompt_hint: Explain a lung nodule calmly. Say it is a small spot in the lung, most are benign (caused by old infections), but doctors will monitor it with repeat scans to ensure it doesn't change.
follow_up: Fleischner Society guidelines for follow-up CT

urdu_hindi: کیلشیم جمع ہونا / कैल्शियम का जमाव
patient_explanation: There is calcium build-up in the tissue or blood vessel.
clinical_context: Calcification can occur with aging or previous inflammation.
action_note: This is often a chronic or older finding.
ollama_prompt_hint: Explain calcification reassuringly. Say calcium has deposited in tissue over time, similar to chalk forming, often from old injuries or infections. It is usually benign.
follow_up: Usually no action required unless in a blood vessel

urdu_hindi: جوڑوں کی خرابی / जोड़ों की टूट-फूट
patient_explanation: The scan shows changes that commonly happen over time with aging or joint use.
clinical_context: Often seen in joints or the spine.
action_note: A doctor can explain if treatment or exercises may help.
ollama_prompt_hint: Explain degenerative changes simply. Tell the patient these are normal wear-and-tear changes that come with age in the joints or spine, and that pain management and physiotherapy can help.
follow_up: Physiotherapy; pain management; orthopaedic review if severe

urdu_hindi: معمول کے مطابق / सामान्य दिखना
patient_explanation: The scanned area looks normal and no significant problem was seen.
clinical_context: Often used in radiology reports to describe normal findings.
action_note: This is generally reassuring.
ollama_prompt_hint: Reassure the patient that 'unremarkable' is good news — it means the organ or area looks completely normal on imaging.
follow_up: No action required

urdu_hindi: بڑی گانٹھ / बड़ी गांठ
patient_explanation: A larger abnormal lump or growth was seen on the scan.
clinical_context: A mass can have many causes and does not automatically mean cancer.
action_note: This should be discussed with a healthcare professional.
ollama_prompt_hint: Explain a mass finding clearly but not alarmingly. Say a growth larger than 3cm was seen, it requires further evaluation like biopsy, and the cause may be benign or need treatment.
follow_up: Biopsy or further imaging often required

urdu_hindi: پھیپھڑے میں غیر معمولی مواد / फेफड़े में असामान्य सामग्री
patient_explanation: Part of the lung shows changes that may be due to infection, fluid, or inflammation.
clinical_context: Commonly mentioned in chest imaging when pneumonia or inflammation is suspected.
action_note: A doctor can explain whether treatment or follow-up is needed.
ollama_prompt_hint: Explain lung infiltrate simply. Say the X-ray shows an area in the lung that is hazy or cloudy, suggesting infection, fluid, or inflammation, and further tests will identify the cause.
follow_up: Cultures; antibiotics if infective; repeat imaging

urdu_hindi: پھیپھڑوں کا حد سے زیادہ پھولنا / फेफड़ों का अत्यधिक फूलना
patient_explanation: The lungs appear more inflated than usual on the scan.
clinical_context: This may be seen in conditions that affect breathing, such as COPD or asthma.
action_note: This should be interpreted with breathing symptoms and medical history.
ollama_prompt_hint: Explain hyperinflation clearly. Say the lungs look bigger than normal on the X-ray, which can happen when air gets trapped — common in COPD or asthma — and inhalers can help.
follow_up: Pulmonary function tests; respiratory physician review

urdu_hindi: پھیپھڑوں کی جھلی کا موٹا ہونا / फेफड़े की झिल्ली का मोटा होना
patient_explanation: The lining around the lungs appears thicker than usual.
clinical_context: This can occur after infection, inflammation, or previous exposure/injury.
action_note: A healthcare professional can explain if follow-up is required.
ollama_prompt_hint: Explain pleural thickening simply. Say the lining around the lungs has become thicker, often from old infections or inflammation, and is usually harmless but needs monitoring.
follow_up: Occupational history important; CT for detailed evaluation

simple_meaning: Damage to air spaces in the lungs
urdu_hindi: پھیپھڑوں کی ہوا کی تھیلیوں کا پھٹنا / फेफड़ों में हवा की थैलियों का नष्ट होना
patient_explanation: The scan shows changes in the lungs that can make breathing less efficient.
clinical_context: Often related to long-term lung irritation or smoking history.
action_note: Discuss this with a healthcare professional, especially if there are breathing symptoms.
ollama_prompt_hint: Explain emphysema carefully. Say the small air sacs in the lungs have been damaged and merged into larger, less efficient spaces — commonly from smoking — making breathing harder over time.
follow_up: Pulmonology referral; smoking cessation; bronchodilators

urdu_hindi: پھیپھڑوں میں پانی / फेफड़ों में पानी
patient_explanation: There is extra fluid in the lung tissue.
clinical_context: This may be related to heart or lung conditions.
action_note: This should be reviewed promptly by a healthcare professional.
ollama_prompt_hint: Explain pulmonary edema urgently. Say fluid has leaked into the lungs making it hard to breathe, often from heart failure, and it needs prompt treatment with diuretics and oxygen.
follow_up: Urgent cardiology assessment; diuretics; oxygen

urdu_hindi: دماغ کے مرکز سے ہٹنا / मस्तिष्क का केंद्र से खिसकना
patient_explanation: Some brain structures appear pushed away from their usual position.
clinical_context: This can happen when there is swelling, bleeding, or pressure inside the skull.
action_note: This usually needs urgent medical review.
ollama_prompt_hint: Explain midline shift urgently. Say pressure inside the skull is pushing brain structures to one side, which is a serious sign requiring urgent neurosurgical evaluation.
follow_up: Neurosurgical emergency; ICP monitoring

urdu_hindi: خون کی کمی / खून की कमी
patient_explanation: An area may not be getting enough blood supply.
clinical_context: In brain imaging, this can relate to stroke-like changes.
action_note: This should be reviewed by a healthcare professional.
ollama_prompt_hint: Explain ischemia urgently. Say an area of the brain is not getting enough blood, which can lead to a stroke if untreated, and immediate medical assessment is critical.
follow_up: Stroke team assessment; clot-busting medication if within window

simple_meaning: Tissue damage from reduced blood supply
urdu_hindi: خون بند ہونے سے نقصان / रक्त की कमी से ऊतक क्षति
patient_explanation: An area of tissue may have been damaged because of reduced blood flow.
clinical_context: In brain reports, this often refers to stroke-related damage.
action_note: A doctor can explain whether it is new or old and what it means.
ollama_prompt_hint: Explain infarct clearly. Say a stroke has caused permanent damage to a small area of brain tissue due to loss of blood supply. Old infarcts are from past events; new ones are emergencies.
follow_up: Neurology; anticoagulation or antiplatelet therapy

simple_meaning: Shrinkage or loss of tissue volume
urdu_hindi: ٹشو کا سکڑنا / ऊतक का सिकुड़ना
patient_explanation: Some tissue appears smaller than expected.
clinical_context: In brain imaging, mild atrophy can be age-related.
action_note: A doctor can explain if this is expected for age or needs follow-up.
ollama_prompt_hint: Explain brain atrophy gently. Say that the brain shows some reduction in size, which is normal with aging, but if more than expected for age, a neurologist may do further tests for memory conditions.
follow_up: Neuropsychological assessment if symptomatic

urdu_hindi: ہڈی کی نوک / हड्डी की नोक
patient_explanation: Small bony outgrowths have formed at the edges of a joint.
clinical_context: These are common in arthritic joints and the spine.
action_note: Usually age-related; physiotherapy or pain management may help.
ollama_prompt_hint: Explain osteophytes simply. Say that extra bone has grown along joint edges as a response to wear and tear. They can cause pain if they press on nerves but are usually manageable.
follow_up: Pain management; physiotherapy; rarely surgery

urdu_hindi: ریڑھ کی ہڈی کی ڈسک کا کھسکنا / रीढ़ की हड्डी की डिस्क का खिसकना
patient_explanation: One of the cushioning discs between spinal bones has bulged out of place.
clinical_context: This can press on spinal nerves causing pain, numbness, or weakness.
action_note: Neurosurgical or orthopaedic review may be needed depending on symptoms.
ollama_prompt_hint: Explain disc herniation clearly. Say the soft cushion between two spinal bones has slipped out and may be pressing on a nerve, causing back or leg pain, and most cases improve with physiotherapy.
follow_up: Physiotherapy; pain management; surgery if neurological deficit

urdu_hindi: دماغ میں پانی / दिमाग में पानी
patient_explanation: Too much cerebrospinal fluid has built up inside the brain's cavities.
clinical_context: This increases pressure inside the skull and can cause serious symptoms.
action_note: This usually requires urgent neurosurgical assessment.
ollama_prompt_hint: Explain hydrocephalus clearly. Say there is too much fluid inside the cavities of the brain, which builds up pressure and can cause headaches, vomiting, or confusion — often treated with a shunt.
follow_up: Neurosurgical review; ventriculoperitoneal shunt

urdu_hindi: ہڈی کا گھلنا / हड्डी का घुलना
patient_explanation: An area of bone appears to be breaking down or being eaten away.
clinical_context: This can be caused by cancer spreading to bone, infections, or certain bone diseases.
action_note: This needs urgent follow-up with a specialist.
ollama_prompt_hint: Explain a lytic lesion carefully. Say an area of bone is being destroyed, which can happen from cancer spread, infection, or certain bone conditions, and urgent further tests are needed.
follow_up: Oncology; bone biopsy; whole body staging

urdu_hindi: پھیپھڑے میں دھندلا پن / फेफड़े में धुंधलापन
patient_explanation: Part of the lung looks hazy, like frosted glass — the structures underneath can still be seen.
clinical_context: Seen in infections, inflammation, or early lung disease. Was widely reported in COVID-19 chest CTs.
action_note: Follow-up imaging or clinical correlation needed.
ollama_prompt_hint: Explain ground glass opacity clearly. Say part of the lung looks like frosted glass on the scan — hazy but you can see through it. It often means infection or inflammation and needs clinical correlation.
follow_up: Repeat CT in 3-6 months if no infection; PFTs

urdu_hindi: لمف نوڈ کا بڑھنا / लिम्फ नोड्स का बढ़ना
patient_explanation: The lymph nodes (small glands in the body) appear larger than normal.
clinical_context: Can be due to infection, inflammation, or in some cases cancer.
action_note: The cause needs to be determined with further tests.
ollama_prompt_hint: Explain lymphadenopathy gently. Say the lymph nodes (small glands) are swollen, which commonly happens with infections but can also signal other conditions — a doctor will determine the cause.
follow_up: Blood tests; biopsy if persistent or large
